name: Triple Sloths v2
description: 8HP NLC triple chaos. Torpor (~20s), Apathy (~75s) and Inertia (~30min) each expose X/Y/Z (Z = inverted Y). A difference rectifier across the three Zs adds + and − outs. 11 outputs, 2 CV ins.

controls.name: Torpor
controls.description: Biases the Torpor chaos circuit's weight between its two strange attractors. It is not a frequency/speed control — it shifts which attractor the signal dwells near.

controls.name: Apathy
controls.description: Biases the Apathy chaos circuit's weight between its two strange attractors. Like Torpor, it is a weight control rather than a rate control.

inputs.name: Torpor CV
inputs.description: CV input injected into the Torpor chaos circuit. Modulation may ride on the chaos smoothly or — depending on conditions — cause windows of periodicity or sudden voltage jumps.

inputs.name: Apathy CV
inputs.description: CV input injected into the Apathy chaos circuit. Same behavior as Torpor CV — may add smooth motion, periodic windows, or discrete jumps.

outputs.name: Torpor X
outputs.description: Torpor section output taken from one stage of the chaos circuit. Moves on the order of ~15–30 seconds per trip around the two attractors.

outputs.name: Torpor Y
outputs.description: Torpor section output from a different stage of the circuit — a related but distinct chaotic signal.

outputs.name: Torpor Z
outputs.description: Inverted copy of Torpor Y. Also summed into the difference rectifier with opposite sign from Apathy/Inertia Z.

outputs.name: Apathy X
outputs.description: Apathy section output from one stage. Roughly ~60–90 seconds per cycle around the attractors.

outputs.name: Apathy Y
outputs.description: Apathy section output from a second stage — different character from X.

outputs.name: Apathy Z
outputs.description: Inverted copy of Apathy Y. Summed (positive) into the difference rectifier.

outputs.name: Inertia X
outputs.description: Inertia section output from one stage. The slowest of the three — roughly 30–40 minutes for a full traverse. No front-panel control.

outputs.name: Inertia Y
outputs.description: Inertia section output from a second stage. Glacial motion — barely perceptible change on short timescales.

outputs.name: Inertia Z
outputs.description: Inverted copy of Inertia Y. Summed (positive) into the difference rectifier.

outputs.name: + (Difference Rectifier)
outputs.description: Positive half of the difference rectifier applied to (VApathy_Z + VInertia_Z − VTorpor_Z). Outputs only when the sum is positive.

outputs.name: − (Difference Rectifier)
outputs.description: Negative half of the difference rectifier applied to (VApathy_Z + VInertia_Z − VTorpor_Z). Outputs only when the sum is negative.

behaviors.name: Three radically different time scales
behaviors.trigger: patch X/Y/Z from all three sections
behaviors.description: Torpor traverses its attractors in ~15–30 seconds, Apathy in ~60–90 seconds, Inertia in ~30–40 minutes. This gives you three layers of modulation — 'fast' LFO-ish wander, mid-scale drift, and geological creep — from one 8HP module.

behaviors.name: Weight, not frequency
behaviors.description: The knobs bias the chaotic system's weight toward one attractor or the other. You can coax the signal to 'dwell' near one lobe but you can't speed it up — the time scale is set by the analog components inside.

behaviors.name: CV injection → periodic windows or jumps
behaviors.trigger: patch CV into Torpor CV or Apathy CV
behaviors.description: External CV rides on the chaos. Depending on amplitude and the current attractor state, you may get smooth added motion, bursts of periodicity (the chaos locks temporarily into a repeating loop), or discrete voltage jumps as the system flips between lobes.

behaviors.name: Inertia is uncontrollable by design
behaviors.trigger: leave Inertia untouched (no pot, no CV in)
behaviors.description: Inertia has no front-panel control and no CV input. It simply drifts on its own ~30–40 minute cycle. Patch Inertia X/Y/Z for 'set and forget' very-slow modulation you cannot rush.

behaviors.name: Z is inverted Y
behaviors.trigger: compare any section's Y and Z outputs
behaviors.description: Within each section, Z is a hard-wired inverted copy of Y. Useful for complementary stereo modulation or for pushing/pulling two destinations against each other without a dedicated inverter.

behaviors.name: Difference rectifier on the three Z outputs
behaviors.description: The three Z outputs are summed with Torpor Z flipped: (Apathy_Z + Inertia_Z − Torpor_Z). The positive half appears at +, the negative half at −. Combines three time scales into one rectified CV whose shape depends on all three sloths simultaneously.

patchIdeas.name: Three-rate mod bank (one voice, three timescales)
patchIdeas.patch: Torpor X → filter cutoff · Apathy Y → VCA CV · Inertia Z → VCO FM — one voice breathes on three unrelated timescales.
steps: Set Torpor to ~12:00 and Apathy to ~12:00 (neutral weight for both).
Leave TORPOR CV and APATHY CV unpatched.
Patch Triple Sloths TORPOR X → filter CUTOFF (timbre shifts every ~20–30 s).
Patch Triple Sloths APATHY Y → VCA CV (loudness drifts over ~1–1.5 min).
Patch Triple Sloths INERTIA Z → VCO FM input (slow detune over tens of minutes).
Patch VCO → filter → VCA → mixer. Let it run — come back in 5 minutes and the sound will have moved without you touching anything.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Three parallel eurorack-level CV streams (Torpor X, Apathy Y, Inertia Z) modulating cutoff, VCA and FM simultaneously.
patchIdeas.hear: A patch that never sits still: fastest wobble on the filter, slower volume swells, and an almost imperceptible long-term drift of pitch — three layers of life from one module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: ~12:00
* Apathy: ~12:00

VCO:

Filter:

VCA:

- Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor X) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy Y) >> VCA (CV)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Inertia Z) >> VCO (FM)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Quantized chaos melody
patchIdeas.patch: Apathy X → quantizer → VCO V/Oct · Torpor Z → filter cutoff · a wandering-but-musical line with independent timbre drift.
steps: Set Apathy to ~1:00 (biases toward one attractor — limits pitch range).
Set Torpor to ~11:00.
Patch Triple Sloths APATHY X → quantizer IN.
Patch quantizer OUT → VCO V/OCT.
Patch Triple Sloths TORPOR Z → filter CUTOFF.
Trigger a slow envelope per note (tap tempo or external clock → VCA).
Rotate APATHY to re-center the pitch cloud; rotate TORPOR to shift the filter sweep's bias.
patchIdeas.signalOut: APATHY X (0–~5V chaos CV) into the quantizer; quantized pitch CV to VCO; TORPOR Z as independent filter CV.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow unpredictable melody in the quantizer's scale — notes arrive every tens of seconds, with the filter opening and closing on its own unrelated rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: ~11:00
* Apathy: ~1:00

Quantizer:

VCO:

Filter:

VCA:

- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy X) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor Z) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Difference-rectifier accent pair
patchIdeas.patch: + output → accent/decay CV, − output → complementary VCA CV — one chaos module as a two-sided dynamics controller.
steps: Set Torpor to ~12:00 and Apathy to ~12:00 for balanced attractor weight.
Patch Triple Sloths + OUTPUT → envelope DECAY CV (or directly into a VCA on a drum/pluck).
Patch Triple Sloths − OUTPUT → a second VCA CV (opposing channel — e.g. a pad or noise).
Trigger a drum or pluck normally.
Observe how + accents one channel while − accents the other, driven by (Apathy_Z + Inertia_Z − Torpor_Z).
Slowly move TORPOR or APATHY to change which side dominates over time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: + OUT: positive half of the summed Z-difference. − OUT: negative half of the same sum. Together they form a chaotic push/pull pair.
patchIdeas.hear: Two elements of the mix trade prominence unpredictably — louder claps against softer hats one minute, reversed the next, all from one module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: ~12:00
* Apathy: ~12:00

Drum A:

Drum B:

- Triple Sloths v2 (+) >> VCA-A (CV)
- Triple Sloths v2 (-) >> VCA-B (CV)
- Drum A (Out) -> VCA-A (In)
- Drum B (Out) -> VCA-B (In)
- VCA-A (Out) -> Mixer (L)
- VCA-B (Out) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo drift (Y vs Z)
patchIdeas.patch: Apathy Y → left filter cutoff, Apathy Z → right filter cutoff — the inverted pair opens one side as it closes the other.
steps: Set Apathy to ~12:00 for balanced motion.
Patch a mono source into a stereo filter (or two identical filters panned hard L/R).
Patch Triple Sloths APATHY Y → filter LEFT CUTOFF.
Patch Triple Sloths APATHY Z → filter RIGHT CUTOFF (Z = inverted Y).
Optionally patch Inertia X → both cutoffs via attenuated mult for a long-term center shift.
Leave it running — the image breathes left/right over ~1–1.5 minutes.
patchIdeas.signalOut: APATHY Y and APATHY Z — the same signal and its inverse, feeding opposite filter channels.
patchIdeas.hear: A stereo image that slowly rocks side to side: as the left filter opens, the right closes, and back again — never exactly repeating.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Apathy: ~12:00

Filter (stereo):

- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy Y) >> Filter (Cutoff L)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy Z) >> Filter (Cutoff R)
- Source (Out) -> Filter (In L+R)
- Filter (Out L) -> Output (L)
- Filter (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: CV-disturbed chaos (periodicity windows)
patchIdeas.patch: LFO into Torpor CV and Apathy CV — watch the chaos occasionally lock into brief periodic loops, then fall back into drift.
steps: Set Torpor to ~12:00 and Apathy to ~12:00.
Patch an LFO (triangle, ~0.05 Hz — very slow) → Triple Sloths TORPOR CV.
Patch the same LFO (or a second, different rate) → Triple Sloths APATHY CV.
Patch Triple Sloths TORPOR Y → VCO FM (audible destination so you can hear the motion).
Patch VCO → filter → VCA → mixer.
Listen: you should hear mostly smooth drift with occasional brief loops where the chaos repeats itself for a few cycles before breaking free.
patchIdeas.signalOut: TORPOR Y CV — chaotic motion with external LFO riding on top, occasionally forced into periodicity or abrupt jumps.
patchIdeas.hear: A pitch drift that wanders aimlessly most of the time, then unexpectedly settles into a short repeating pattern, then slips back into chaos — the 'windows of periodicity' NLC describes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: ~12:00
* Apathy: ~12:00

LFO:

VCO:

Filter:

VCA:

- LFO (Out) >> Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor CV)
- LFO (Out) >> Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy CV)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor Y) >> VCO (FM)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
